+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-134
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_23-40-55+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs — added `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed`, the `KeyCode::Digit2` mapping, and the handler arm delegating to `self.set_time_scale(2.0)`.
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs — extended `key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` with the Digit2 mapping and `ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound` with Ctrl+Shift+2 / bare-Digit2 cases.
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs — added three Ctrl+2 behavior tests (fresh-shell submit, undo restores DEFAULT, repeat at 2.0 is a no-op).
+STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+EXEC_STATUS: blocked
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_TASK_2026-05-23_23-26-42+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`:
+  - `EditorKeyCommand` gains one new variant: `SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed` (Ctrl+2 → 2.0x time scale via the existing slider path). Doc lines describe that it reuses the existing `SetTimeScale` action and `set_time_scale` no-op short-circuit.
+  - `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` mapping table gains exactly one arm: `KeyCode::Digit2 => Self::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed` inside the existing Ctrl-without-Shift gate, so Ctrl+Shift+2 and bare Digit2 remain unbound by the existing `if !ctrl || shift { return None; }` guard.
+  - `EditorShell::handle_key_command` gains one match arm: `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed => self.set_time_scale(2.0)`. No direct mutation of `TimeScale`, `World`, resources, audit ledgers, or CommandBus internals — strictly delegates to the existing slider entry point.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs::key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` — extended with `assert_eq!(from_key_press(Digit2, true, false), Some(SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed))`.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs::ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound` — extended with `Ctrl+Shift+2 → None` and `bare Digit2 → None` assertions.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_2_on_fresh_shell_sets_time_scale_to_double_speed` — pins Ctrl+2 on a fresh shell drives `time_scale().value()` from 1.0 → 2.0 and pushes exactly one stack entry.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_2_then_undo_restores_time_scale_to_default` — pins Ctrl+Z after Ctrl+2 restores `time_scale().value()` to `TimeScale::DEFAULT` (1.0).
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::repeated_ctrl_2_at_double_speed_is_noop` — pins that a second Ctrl+2 at 2.0 produces no new stack entry, no new `TimeScaleChanged` audit event, and leaves the value at 2.0. Relies on the existing `set_time_scale` no-op short-circuit (no new short-circuit logic added).
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. Scope explicitly forbade docs/ADRs/plans changes.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Skipped — bullet lists above are sufficient.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+Cargo binary located at `A:\RustCache\cargo\bin\cargo.exe` per `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` PATH override (cargo is not on the unattended-session PATH by default).
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (before editing) → no tracked modifications; only the inbound `ISSUE-134_TASK_*.md` + sidecar were untracked. No pre-existing dirty state.
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` → exit 0. Clean compile after the variant + handler additions.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` → exit 0.
+- `cargo clippy -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets -- -D warnings` → **exit 101 (FAIL)**. The failure is in `crates/brep-render/src/lib.rs:306` (`let base = (tri_idx * 3) as u32;`), triggering `clippy::cast_possible_truncation` (a pedantic lint promoted to error by `-D warnings`). This file is **outside** the MAY-edit set and the line dates from commit `426e999e` (2026-05-10), confirmed via `git blame` — not introduced by this dispatch. Fixing it would require editing `crates/brep-render/src/lib.rs`, which is forbidden by the dispatch scope. See "Open Questions for Reviewer" for the structural cause and recommended resolution.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test keyboard_command_bus_round_trip` → exit 0. 10 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (8 pre-existing + 2 in-place extensions to existing tests; no new test functions added in this file per minimal-diff judgment).
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test time_scale_test` → exit 0. 17 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (14 pre-existing + 3 new: `ctrl_2_on_fresh_shell_sets_time_scale_to_double_speed`, `ctrl_2_then_undo_restores_time_scale_to_default`, `repeated_ctrl_2_at_double_speed_is_noop`).
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0. All 10 enforcement checks PASS; 3 supplementary checks PASS (snapshot-participate informational only).
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0. All 7 verification steps passed: `cargo +nightly fmt --check`, architecture lints, architecture-lint test suite, `cargo deny check`, `cargo test --workspace --all-targets -j 1`, `cargo test --workspace --doc -j 1`, `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`. Note: `dispatch.verify.ps1` does NOT run clippy, which is why this in-repo CI mirror passes despite the clippy-only failure above.
+- `git status --short` (after all edits) → Executor-created changes limited to the three allowed edit files plus the EXEC packet (this file) and its sidecar:
+  ```
+   M crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs
+   M crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs
+   M crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_2026-05-23_23-40-55+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_2026-05-23_23-40-55+0300.meta.json   (created by -Finalize)
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_TASK_2026-05-23_23-26-42+0300.md           (inbound, untouched)
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_TASK_2026-05-23_23-26-42+0300.meta.json    (inbound, untouched)
+  ```
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+- The `cargo clippy -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets -- -D warnings` verification gate failed at exit 101 because of a pre-existing `clippy::cast_possible_truncation` lint in `crates/brep-render/src/lib.rs:306` (the workspace's `[workspace.lints.clippy] pedantic = "warn"` plus this gate's `-D warnings` promotes that pedantic lint to a hard error). The failing line is outside the MAY-edit set and was last modified on 2026-05-10 (commit `426e999e`), before this dispatch existed. Per the Task Packet's "If a required verification command fails because of unrelated pre-existing work outside the allowed scope, do not fix outside scope" instruction and the matching halt condition ("Any required verification failure points to code outside the allowed edit set and fixing it would require touching a forbidden file"), the failure is documented here instead of repaired. All other in-scope deliverables and verification gates passed.
+- All other deliverables, scope envelope, and acceptance criteria were met without deviation: one new enum variant, one new mapping arm, one new handler arm, three new focused tests + two in-place test extensions, no production action created, no `Action`/`CommandBus`/state surface touched, no Cargo/doc/script changes, no edit to `lifecycle/mod.rs`, no forbidden file touched.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- **Clippy gate vs. CI mirror divergence.** `dispatch.verify.ps1` (the canonical "CI would pass" gate) does NOT invoke `cargo clippy`, so prior dispatches (e.g. ISSUE-126 on 2026-05-23) did not surface this brep-render pedantic-lint failure. The Task Packet for ISSUE-134 adds `cargo clippy -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets -- -D warnings` as a required gate above the CI mirror, which is what exposed it. The brep-render code is pre-existing and unchanged by this dispatch. Resolution options for the human arbiter / Planner:
+  1. Carve out a follow-up dispatch that fixes `crates/brep-render/src/lib.rs:306` (e.g. via `u32::try_from(tri_idx * 3).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` or a narrow `#[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]` with rationale).
+  2. Relax the workspace `[workspace.lints.clippy] pedantic = "warn"` setting (workspace-policy change — not appropriate for an ISSUE-134 follow-up).
+  3. Accept clippy is not a CI gate today and drop `cargo clippy ... -D warnings` from future Task Packets that mirror `dispatch.verify.ps1`.
+  Option 1 is the lightest and matches existing repo policy.
+- **Naming choice.** I named the enum variant `SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed` exactly as specified in the Task Packet deliverable. Confirm this is the intended user-facing variant name (vs. e.g. `SetTimeScale2x`).
+- **Test placement.** The Digit2 mapping assertion was added to the existing `key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` test rather than as a new standalone test, because the existing test's stated purpose is "the mapping table itself is part of the dispatch contract; this test guards against accidental rebindings or expansions" — extending it preserves that contract scope. Ctrl+Shift+2 and bare-Digit2 unbound assertions were similarly added to `ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound`. If you'd rather have dedicated `digit2_*` test functions for traceability, the split is a 30-second follow-up.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs` (all modified; none staged).
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_TASK_2026-05-23_23-26-42+0300.md` + sidecar (inbound TASK packet, untouched); `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_2026-05-23_23-40-55+0300.md` (this report) + matching `.meta.json` sidecar produced by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`.
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-134`.
+- Last commit: `ed7ebda` chore(dispatch): seed task #25 Ctrl+2 CommandBus action.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-134
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,129 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-134
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_23-26-42+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_*.md
+- GitHub issue #134
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #134: add the smallest Ctrl+2 CommandBus time-scale action in editor-shell. The new binding must map Ctrl+2 to `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed`, and handling that command must call `EditorShell::set_time_scale(2.0)` so the existing `SetTimeScale` action and existing `CommandBus::submit` path provide undo/no-op behavior. This dispatch is intentionally narrow: one command enum variant, one key mapping, one handler arm, and focused tests only.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-state/**`
+- `editor/rge-editor/**`
+- `kernel/ecs/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Any `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifest, dependency declaration, feature flag, or generated Cargo metadata file.
+- Any editor-shell production source file other than `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`.
+- Any test file other than `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- Any docs, ADRs, plans, scripts, schemas, workflows, status files, doctrine files, lint configuration, `.gitignore`, generated artifact, persisted log, or repository metadata file.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet, after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, comment, review, commit, or remote state.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, fixtures, docs, generated artifacts, persisted logs, Cargo entries, dependency files, crates, architecture lints, schemas, scripts, workflow files, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CORRECT packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- `EditorKeyCommand` gains exactly one new production command variant named `SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed`.
+- `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press(KeyCode::Digit2, true, false)` returns `Some(EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed)`.
+- `EditorShell::handle_key_command(EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed)` routes by calling `self.set_time_scale(2.0)`.
+- The implementation uses the existing `SetTimeScale` action and the existing `EditorShell::set_time_scale` -> `submit_action` -> `CommandBus::submit` path.
+- Focused tests cover all required behavior:
+  - Digit2 key mapping for Ctrl-without-Shift.
+  - Fresh-shell Ctrl+2 submit changes `TimeScale` from 1.0 to 2.0.
+  - Undo after Ctrl+2 restores `TimeScale` to 1.0.
+  - Repeating Ctrl+2 while already at 2.0 is a no-op: no new stack entry and no second time-scale audit event.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet documents the exact files changed, verification commands run, results, and any deviations or halt condition.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only production behavior change is Ctrl+2 double-speed time scale through `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed`.
+- No new `Action` trait shape, no new production `Action` implementation, no adapter ledger, no adapter authority layer, and no `CompoundAction` wrapper is introduced.
+- `EditorShell::handle_key_command` does not directly mutate `TimeScale`, `World`, resources, audit ledgers, or CommandBus internals for Ctrl+2; it delegates to `set_time_scale(2.0)`.
+- The existing no-op behavior in `EditorShell::set_time_scale` remains authoritative for repeat Ctrl+2. A second Ctrl+2 at 2.0 must not submit another action, grow the undo stack, flip state beyond the first submit, or record another `TimeScaleChanged` audit event.
+- Existing Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Y, and Ctrl+S mappings and behavior remain unchanged.
+- Ctrl+Shift+2 is unbound unless the current mapping signature makes that impossible without broader input-surface changes; in that case halt with `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The tests are placed only in `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` and/or `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- The implementation compiles without touching any forbidden file or adding Cargo dependencies.
+- The final diff contains no UI, menu, toolbar, egui, editor binary, Cargo, editor-actions, editor-state, ECS, or architecture-doctrine changes.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not add Ctrl+0, Ctrl+1, Ctrl+3, a preset trio, step-up/step-down commands, playback commands, UI, menu items, toolbar buttons, status-bar changes, egui wiring, editor binary event rewrites, or any visible control beyond the existing keyboard command dispatch path.
+- Do not change the `Action` trait, `CommandBus`, `UndoStack`, action payload semantics, coalescing policy, audit-ledger design, editor-state APIs, ECS resource APIs, or architecture-lint policy.
+- Do not add a new production action for double speed. Ctrl+2 must reuse the existing `SetTimeScale` action via `EditorShell::set_time_scale(2.0)`.
+- Do not move command code out of `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`.
+- Do not edit `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`; the existing keyboard dispatch into `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` must remain sufficient.
+- Do not broaden tests into GPU, render-path, egui, editor binary, or workspace-wide behavior beyond the required gates.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these commands in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before editing. Document any pre-existing tracked changes. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo clippy -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test keyboard_command_bus_round_trip` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test time_scale_test` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` -> expected exit 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short` after all edits and gates. Expected Executor-created changes are limited to the three allowed edit files plus the single `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+If a required verification command fails because of behavior inside the allowed files, fix within scope and rerun the failed gate plus any affected focused tests. If a required verification command fails because of unrelated pre-existing work outside the allowed scope, do not fix outside scope; document the failure and halt with the appropriate status described below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without broadening scope and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- `rge_input::KeyCode::Digit2` is unavailable or cannot be referenced from `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` without editing forbidden files.
+- An existing Ctrl+2 binding is present in the current editor-shell command surface and adding this binding would shadow or change it.
+- The command surface has moved enough that implementing Ctrl+2 requires editing `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, editor binary event code, egui/menu/toolbar code, editor-state, editor-actions, ECS, Cargo files, or any other forbidden surface.
+- Routing Ctrl+2 through `EditorShell::set_time_scale(2.0)` would not use the existing `SetTimeScale` action and existing `CommandBus::submit` path.
+- Passing the required tests requires adding a new production `Action` implementation, changing the `Action` trait, creating an adapter ledger, wrapping in `CompoundAction`, or changing CommandBus internals.
+- A required test cannot be written in `keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` or `time_scale_test.rs` without new helper crates, new fixtures, GPU/render setup, or forbidden file edits.
+- The repeat Ctrl+2 no-op behavior cannot be preserved with the existing `set_time_scale` no-op short-circuit.
+- Any required verification failure points to code outside the allowed edit set and fixing it would require touching a forbidden file.
+- Any required file is missing, unreadable, or already changed in a way that makes the bounded implementation unsafe to perform without human review.
+- The Executor accidentally edits a forbidden file or creates a forbidden artifact. Stop immediately, do not continue expanding the diff, and report the deviation.
+
+If a halt condition fires before code changes are safe, write the single EXEC report if possible with header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, include `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and `EXIT_CODE: 0`. If a narrow missing-context blocker is resolvable by the Planner without human architecture judgment, use `STATUS: BLOCKED`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED`, and `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` instead.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The relevant current shape is already in `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`: `SetTimeScale` exists, `EditorShell::set_time_scale` captures `from`, clamps `to`, short-circuits no-ops, submits through `CommandBus`, and records the editor-shell time-scale audit event only after a successful non-no-op submit. This dispatch should add a command entry point to that existing path, not invent a second time-scale mutation surface.
+
